questions.prompt: It’s been 6 months since the CIRP was reviewed. What should you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Schedule a walkthrough exercise with key staff

options.label: C
options.text: Assume it’s fine and do nothing

questions.prompt: Other organisations in your sector experienced recent data loss incidents. How do you respond?

options.label: A
options.text: Review case studies and adjust internal controls

questions.prompt: A new vulnerability affecting your software platform is reported. What action do you take?

options.label: A
options.text: Alert IT and confirm patch status

options.label: B
options.text: Wait for someone else to mention it

questions.prompt: A team member asks where incident procedures are stored. What is the best approach?

options.label: A
options.text: Ensure shared, controlled access to updated documentation

options.label: B
options.text: Keep documents saved locally on one manager’s device

questions.prompt: Annual security training is due. What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Deliver updated training with real examples

questions.prompt: You are reviewing business systems. What should be prioritised?

options.label: A
options.text: Identifying critical systems and map where sensitive data flows

options.label: B
options.text: Focus only on the most visible systems

options.label: C
options.text: Assume all systems are equally important

questions.prompt: Your organisation wants to improve evidence readiness. What action supports forensic readiness?

options.label: A
options.text: Confirm logging, backups, and time synchronisation are enabled

options.label: C
options.text: No preparation and deal with the evidence later

questions.prompt: An employee reports suspicious file activity. What should the service desk do?

options.label: A
options.text: Log incident formally and escalate immediately

options.label: B
options.text: Ask them to monitor it for now

questions.prompt: You receive an alert that company data may be posted online. What is your first step?

options.label: A
options.text: Validate the alert and notify the CIRT

questions.prompt: Indicators suggest a confirmed breach. What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Activate the Cyber Incident Response Team (CIRT)

options.label: B
options.text: Wait for senior approval before alerting anyone

questions.prompt: Early evidence is coming in. What is most important?

options.label: A
options.text: Document timeline, systems involved, and data types

questions.prompt: You suspect broader impact. What should you check?

options.label: A
options.text: Logs, alerts, user reports, and monitoring tools

questions.prompt: Systems may contain evidence. What do you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Preserve logs and avoid altering affected systems

questions.prompt: Impact appears significant. What action aligns with policy?
The breach may require investigation. What should be considered?

options.label: C
options.text: Assume internal staff can manage alone

questions.prompt: Impact is unclear. What should happen?

options.label: B
options.text: Ask general admin staff to review

questions.prompt: Leaked data appears online. What should you verify?

options.label: A
options.text: Whether data is real, current, and organisation-owned

questions.prompt: Data compromised is confirmed. What must be assessed?

options.label: A
options.text: Data classification, customer impact, legal risk

questions.prompt: Personal data may be involved. What is key?
Incident severity unclear. What supports consistency?
If personal customer information may have been exposed, what is the safest action?

options.label: A
options.text: Escalate to the organisation’s data protection lead and check if authorities must be informed

options.label: B
options.text: Monitor quietly and hope the issue resolves

options.label: C
options.text: Avoid reporting to protect the company’s image

questions.prompt: Investigation confirms unauthorised access is ongoing and sensitive data may still be leaving the network. What should you do immediately?

options.label: A
options.text: Isolate affected systems to prevent further data loss

options.label: B
options.text: Monitor activity but leave systems connected

questions.prompt: IT proposes disconnecting multiple systems, but this may disrupt business operations. What is the best approach?

options.label: A
options.text: Assess business impact while prioritising stopping the breach

questions.prompt: One department’s computers appear compromised. What action is safest?

options.label: A
options.text: Disconnect affected devices from the network while preserving evidence

options.label: C
options.text: Leave devices online and continue working

questions.prompt: Security tools may not be fully up to date. What should be done?

options.label: A
options.text: Update security tools and run full scans across systems

questions.prompt: Customers are starting to ask questions about possible service disruption. How should leadership respond?

options.label: A
options.text: Provide controlled, clear communication to stakeholders

options.label: B
options.text: Provide minimal information to avoid concern

questions.prompt: IT confirms malware presence on several machines. What is the most reliable recovery method?

options.label: A
options.text: Rebuild affected systems from trusted backups

options.label: C
options.text: Resume work without full system checks

questions.prompt: The attacker demands payment in exchange for deleting stolen data. What should the organisation do?

options.label: A
options.text: Consult legal and security experts before any response

questions.prompt: Systems appear stable and containment is complete. How should services be restored?

options.label: A
options.text: Gradually restore systems with validation testing

options.label: C
options.text: Skip validation checks to save time

questions.prompt: The incident has been resolved. What documentation is required?

options.label: A
options.text: Produce a structured report detailing timeline, impact, and response

questions.prompt: Teams are returning to normal questions. What should happen next?
Senior management requests cost analysis. What should be included?

options.label: A
options.text: Assess direct, indirect, and reputational costs

questions.prompt: The organisation wants to prevent recurrence. What action is most effective?

options.label: A
options.text: Update policies, controls, and training based on findings

options.label: C
options.text: Assume the issue will not recur

questions.prompt: Employees are aware an incident occurred. How should leadership respond?

options.label: A
options.text: Share clear guidance and reinforce security expectations

options.label: B
options.text: Share limited details with managers only

questions.prompt: Weaknesses were identified in procedures. What should be done?
Improvements have been made to the response plan. What should happen next?

options.label: A
options.text: Run a simulation or tabletop exercise
